49	
50	**Rev 4 design pivot (per user direction 2026-05-03):**
51	
52	**Rev 4 design pivot (per user direction 2026-05-03):**
53	"Workers should always be autonomous. If they have a server to talk to,
54	they send results there. They should be able to do their own work and
55	store their logs and artifacts locally without regard for the server.
56	The server is strictly a value-added coordinator."
57	
58	This shifts the decision from Proposal A (server orchestrates; --local has
59	in-process API impl) to a Proposal B-shaped design: workers are always
60	autonomous against the bead store; server is an optional reporting target
61	that provides centralized observability, cross-worker visibility, and UI
62	surfaces — but is NOT required for correct operation. `--local` becomes
63	"force standalone mode; don't try to reach a server" — the same code
64	path that workers take when no server is reachable.
65	
66	Rev 4 removes most of rev 3's machinery as no longer needed:
67	- No authoritative worker registration, heartbeat, or claim-lease API
68	  (there IS a thin identity-envelope POST for correlation; workers
69	  claim from the bead store atomically as today)
70	- No long-poll `next-bead` endpoint (workers pick from the bead store)
71	- No server-side picker (worker-side picker continues, with the
72	  diagnostic events from commit `80f51574` preserved)
73	- No server-side runtime registry as authoritative state (server's view
74	  is derived from worker event reports — eventually-consistent best-effort)
75	- No claim reconciliation across server restarts (server has no claims
76	  to reconcile)
77	- No partition recovery semantics (worker autonomy makes partition
78	  irrelevant)
79	- No data migration story (per user: delete history if needed; clean
80	  slate is fine)
81

126	**Rev 2 amendments:** added Threat model section; added Picker section;
127	expanded `register` payload; added claim-lease-renewal + terminate

131	added Test Transport roadmap step; clarified FEAT-008 reference.
132	**Layout note:** The bead description suggested `docs/helix/03-decide/`. The
133	HELIX layout in this repo places ADRs at `docs/helix/02-design/adr/`. This ADR
134	is filed in the actual location to keep the index discoverable; the next ADR
135	number after ADR-021 is ADR-022.
136	
137	## Context
138	
139	Operators have observed a structural failure mode: when `ddx-server`
140	restarts, in-flight worker subprocesses lose their connection to the
141	orchestrator. Today there are two execution paths that diverge in lifecycle,
142	configuration plumbing, and observability:
143	
144	- **`--local` path.** `ddx work` (a.k.a. `ddx agent execute-loop`) runs an
145	  in-process drain loop that talks to the bead store directly
146	  (`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`).
147	- **Server-spawned path.** The server forks a worker process, hand-marshals
148	  an `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` over an internal contract, and tracks the PID
149	  in `.ddx/workers/`. The worker reaches back into the bead store directly,
150	  not through the server.
151	
152	Three open beads diagnose the symptom-space:
153	
154	- `ddx-29058e2a` — five flags silently dropped on the server path because
155	  the spec is hand-maintained as parallel structs across six layers.
156	- `ddx-5cb6e6cd` — refactor epic that splits `execute_bead_loop` into
157	  `run/`, `try/`, `work/` packages, exposing an implicit state machine
158	  inside a 700-line function.
159	- `ddx-dc157075` — workers exit prematurely on an empty queue because
160	  `--poll-interval=0` is the default and the per-Run `attempted` map is
161	  scoped to one drain pass.
162	
163	`ddx-4c51d33e` (closed) demonstrated the related security problem: any
164	worker that touches the bead store directly has to reimplement project
165	scoping; the GraphQL resolver got that wrong for `DocumentByPath`.
166	
167	The common cause: **the worker is coupled to local in-process state and
168	the server has no first-class concept of a worker beyond a PID it forked.**
169	Restart loses the in-flight context because the context lives nowhere
170	addressable. Each fix to one of the three beads above fixes a leaf without
171	addressing the trunk.
172	
173	This ADR exists because the next round of refactors (the `try/` and `work/`
174	packages, the `ExecuteLoopSpec` unification, the stay-alive fix) will all
175	re-cement the current bipartite design unless the architectural shift is
176	agreed first.
177	
178	## Decision
179	
180	**Workers are always autonomous. There is no "--local mode" — the
181	worker has one mode (autonomous) and detects a server continuously. If
182	a server is reachable, the worker emits events and results to it
183	best-effort. If no server is reachable (none running, partition, never
184	configured), the worker proceeds with its work and tries again later.
185	The bead store is the source of truth; the server is an optional
186	observability + coordination target.**
187	
188	Concretely:
189	
190	1. A worker process (`ddx work`) reads the bead store directly, picks
191	   the next eligible bead per the current picker logic (priority asc,
192	   FIFO within tier), claims it atomically, executes via `try.Attempt`,
193	   writes evidence locally, and reports the result by writing the bead's
194	   event log + closing the bead via the store.
195	2. **All of #1 happens regardless of whether a server is running.**
196	   This is the existing autonomous behavior; the worker has no
197	   dependency on a server for correctness.
198	3. **In parallel**, a small "server probe" goroutine inside the worker
199	   periodically (every N seconds, default 30) checks
200	   `~/.local/share/ddx/server.addr` (XDG-compliant, matches current code) for a reachable server. State transitions:
201	   - **NotConnected → Connected:** server appeared; worker POSTs
202	     `register` with a thin identity envelope (project root, harness,
203	     executor pid/host); on success, worker stores the `worker_id` and
204	     starts mirroring events to the server best-effort.
205	   - **Connected → Connected:** server still reachable; continue
206	     mirroring.
207	   - **Connected → NotConnected:** server unreachable (crash,
208	     partition, stopped); worker logs the transition, stops trying to
209	     mirror events, retains its bead-store-local event log, continues
210	     working.
211	   - **NotConnected → NotConnected:** still no server; nothing to do.
212	4. When in Connected state, the worker mirrors bead events + final
213	   results to `/api/workers/<id>/event` and the bead-close result to
214	   the same endpoint best-effort. Failures (server crash mid-POST,
215	   partition, 5xx) are logged locally and ignored — the bead's local
216	   event log is authoritative.
217	5. The server's view (which workers exist, which beads are running,
218	   recent events) is **derived from worker reports** — eventually
219	   consistent, NOT authoritative. The server's value is centralized
220	   observability, cross-worker dashboards, operator UI, and
221	   server-initiated cancel (which writes a marker to the bead store
222	   that workers honor on next iteration).
223	6. Server restart drops the in-memory derived view; on next probe cycle
224	   the worker re-registers (or notices the server is gone). No
225	   in-flight work is at risk because workers don't depend on the
226	   server.
227	
228	**`--local` flag is removed.** Today's `--local` semantics are the
229	default behavior; there's nothing to opt into. Operators who pass
230	`--local` see a deprecation warning + the flag is a no-op (no
231	"force standalone" behavior — the worker probes for a server normally;
232	operators wanting to suppress the probe can stop the server). After one
233	alpha release with the deprecation warning, the flag is deleted. The
234	breaking-changes section documents this.
235	
236	This is **Proposal B-shaped** (server is passive observer) with one
237	addition: the server can write back to the bead store to influence
238	worker decisions (e.g., cancel a bead by setting an extra field; set
239	priority overrides). Workers see those changes on next bead-store read.
240	
241	### Why autonomous-default workers
242	
243	- **Workers don't depend on the server for correctness.** Any server
244	  restart, partition, crash, or just "no server running" is a no-op for
245	  the worker's primary job (executing beads).
246	- **Same code path for every invocation.** `ddx work` and `ddx work
247	  --local` differ only in whether they bother trying to reach a server.
248	  No in-process API server, no Transport interface, no test-mode
249	  fixture — the existing unit-test setup (no server) is the autonomous
250	  case.
251	- **Server is opt-in observability.** Operators who want centralized
252	  dashboards run a server; operators who don't, don't. Either is
253	  correct.
254	- **No claim coordination races.** The bead store's atomic claim is the
255	  only claim primitive. Two workers contending for the same bead use
256	  the same store-level CAS that exists today; there is no parallel
257	  server-side claim table to race against.
258	- **Project boundaries enforced by the existing per-project bead store.**
259	  Each `.ddx/beads.jsonl` is a project; workers operate against one
260	  project's store; the cross-project leak class (`ddx-4c51d33e`) is
261	  prevented at the worker level because workers never read another
262	  project's store.
263	- **`ExecuteLoopSpec` from `ddx-29058e2a` still applies** — but as a
264	  worker-side struct unification (cobra → in-process flow), not a wire
265	  format. The drop class is closed by the same single-struct
266	  discipline; it just doesn't need to cross a network boundary.
267	
268	### Threat model (limits of session-token project binding)
269	
270	Session tokens prevent **honest worker mixup** (a misconfigured worker for
271	project A cannot accidentally claim a bead from project B). They are NOT a
272	defense against a **local malicious process**: `requireTrusted` accepts any
273	loopback connection without identity proof, so any process on the same host
274	can register as any project. The session-token model is project-scoping for
275	legitimate workers, not authentication of worker identity. Future work to
276	strengthen this (e.g., per-worker keypairs, ts-net WhoIs binding for
277	loopback registrations) is out of scope for v1 and tracked separately.
278	
279	### Picker (worker-side, status quo with diagnostic events)
280	
281	The bead picker stays where it lives today: in the worker, against the
282	bead store. The fix at commit `80f51574` added `picker.priority_skip`
283	and `picker.claim_race` diagnostic events; both are preserved. Picker
284	rules (unchanged from current code):
285	
286	1. Read eligible beads via `Store.ReadyExecution()` (excludes superseded,
287	   not-execution-eligible, in retry-cooldown).
288	2. Sort by priority ascending (P0 first), then by `updated_at` ascending
289	   (FIFO within tier).
290	3. If `--label-filter` is set, intersect with bead labels.
291	4. Skip beads in the per-Run `attempted` map (set after each unsuccessful
292	   claim attempt; reset on falling through to "no candidate" per the
293	   stay-alive fix at commit `41cb762e`).
294	5. Claim atomically via `Store.Claim()`; on CAS loss, emit
295	   `picker.claim_race` and continue to next eligible.
296	
297	Two workers contending for the same top bead use the bead store's
298	existing atomic claim — the same primitive that has worked correctly
299	for as long as DDx has had a bead store. There is no parallel server-side
300	claim table to race against.
301	
302	When the server is reachable, the worker mirrors the
303	`picker.priority_skip` and `picker.claim_race` events to the server's
304	ingestion endpoint so operators can see them in the UI. When no server,
305	the events still land in the bead's local event stream.
306	
307	### Why not centralized server-side picker (the rev 1-3 design)
308	
309	- Workers don't need it for correctness — the bead store's atomic claim
310	  is sufficient. Adding a server-side claim table creates a parallel
311	  source of truth that has to be reconciled.
312	- Server-side picker requires workers to depend on the server, which
313	  contradicts the autonomous-default decision.
314	- Diagnostic events from commit `80f51574` already make starvation
315	  observable; centralization didn't add visibility we lacked.
316	
317	### Why not Proposal A (server orchestrates, in-process API for --local)
318	
319	(Earlier revisions chose this; rev 4 reverses.)
320	
321	- The in-process API server for `--local` was complexity in service of a
322	  uniformity that the user explicitly doesn't need. `--local` working
323	  *without* a server is the natural and operator-expected behavior.
324	- "Server is the source of truth for runtime state" requires server
325	  restart recovery, claim reconciliation, partition handling, and a
326	  long-poll back-pressure regime — none of which are needed when the
327	  bead store is the source of truth.
328	- Workers being long-lived clients of a server requires the server to be
329	  available for the worker to function, which the user explicitly
330	  rejected.
331	
332	## Worker-server interface (best-effort reporting)
333	
334	The worker-server boundary is small. There is no HTTP API for claim
335	coordination, no long-poll, no heartbeat lease, no in-process server.
336	There are exactly two endpoints, both used best-effort by workers and
337	both backed by `requireTrusted` (loopback or ts-net per ADR-006):
338	
339	### POST /api/workers/register
340	
341	Worker-side: called by the server-probe goroutine on every
342	NotConnected → Connected transition (NOT just at startup). On
343	re-connect after server crash, the worker re-registers and gets a new
344	`worker_id`. Discovery uses
345	`~/.local/share/ddx/server.addr` (XDG-compliant, matches current code)
346	discovery. Body is a small identity envelope:
347	
348	```json
349	{
350	  "project_root": "/abs/path",
351	  "harness": "claude",
352	  "model": "",
353	  "executor_pid": 12345,
354	  "executor_host": "host.local",
355	  "started_at": "2026-05-03T03:00:00Z"
356	}
357	```
358	
359	Response:
360	
361	```json
362	{ "worker_id": "wkr-9f3a..." }
363	```
364	
365	The worker uses `worker_id` as a correlation key in subsequent event
366	reports. If registration fails (no server, network error, 5xx), the
367	worker proceeds without a `worker_id` and skips event mirroring. No
368	session token, no heartbeat, no claim lease — none of those concepts
369	exist in the worker-server protocol because the worker doesn't depend
370	on the server.
371	
372	### POST /api/workers/<id>/event
373	
374	Worker-side: called best-effort whenever the worker would write an event
375	to a bead's local event log. Body mirrors the bead event:
376	
377	```json
378	{
379	  "bead_id": "ddx-076147ee",
380	  "attempt_id": "20260503T021424-dac100b6",
381	  "kind": "attempt.started" | "picker.priority_skip" | "loop.idle" | "result" | "...",
382	  "body": { "...": "..." }
383	}
384	```
385	
386	Server appends to its derived view (in-memory + optionally backed by
387	its own `.ddx/server/events.jsonl`). Response: 204. Worker's local
388	event log is the authoritative copy; server-side is for observability.
389	
390	If the POST fails for any reason, the worker logs the failure locally
391	and continues. Lost events are tolerable because the bead's local event
392	stream has the full record; operators wanting to backfill can replay
393	from the bead store.
394	
395	### What the server can do (the value-add)
396	
397	Once the server has a derived view of who's reporting:
398	
399	- **Centralized observability**: which workers exist, what they're doing
400	  right now, recent picker decisions, recent dropped commits — all
401	  surfaced via existing GraphQL and the workers panel.
402	- **Cross-worker correlation**: see all workers across all projects in
403	  one place; useful for multi-project hosts.
404	- **Operator UI**: queue beads, cancel beads (via writing markers to the
405	  bead store that workers honor on next iteration), view bead history.
406	- **Server-initiated cancel**: not a direct API call to the worker;
407	  instead, server writes `extra.cancel-requested: true` to the bead;
408	  worker observes on next iteration of its loop and aborts the attempt
409	  at the next safe point.
410	
411	### What the server explicitly does NOT do
412	
413	- It does not assign beads to workers (workers pick from the bead store
414	  themselves).
415	- It does not hold authoritative claim state (the bead store does).
416	- It does not gate worker startup or operation (workers run without it).
417	- It does not hold session tokens or enforce per-worker auth (a future
418	  hardening; v1 trusts the requireTrusted boundary).
419	- It does not need to survive worker death gracefully (workers are
420	  independent; server's derived view is replayable from the bead
421	  stores at any time).
422	
423	### Server crash / restart behavior
424	
425	Server crashes: workers' next event POST fails; workers log + continue.
426	Server restarts: workers' next event POST succeeds (or 410 if the
427	server didn't recover the worker_id, in which case the worker
428	re-registers and resumes). No in-flight work is at risk.
429	
430	### Probe + freshness state model
431	
432	Smaller than rev 3's claim/heartbeat model but still needs a single
433	table. The server-probe goroutine:
434	
435	| Event | Threshold | Action |
436	|---|---|---|
437	| Worker process start | immediate (0s) | Probe `~/.local/share/ddx/server.addr`; on success, register |
438	| Probe interval (steady state) | 30s default; 10s min; 5min max; jittered ±20% | Re-check reachability; emit register if NotConnected→Connected; nothing if Connected→Connected |
439	| Connected POST fails (timeout, conn refused, 5xx) | immediate | Worker enters NotConnected; logs locally; continues working |
440	| 5 consecutive probe failures | ~2.5 min default | Worker reduces probe rate to 5min (backoff); resets to 30s on next success |
441	| Server replies 410 unknown_worker | immediate | Worker re-registers within same probe cycle |
442	| Worker process exit (graceful) | immediate | Best-effort POST disconnect; not required for correctness |
443	
444	**Freshness indicators** (server-side, surfaced in the workers panel UI):
445	
446	- `worker_state` ∈ {connected, stale, disconnected}: connected = event
447	  in last 2× probe interval; stale = 2-10× probe interval; disconnected
448	  = > 10× probe interval (worker presumed dead)
449	- `last_event_at`: timestamp of most recent event from this worker
450	- `mirror_failures_count`: total POST failures since worker_id was issued
451	  (operator can see if a worker is healthy but lossy)
452	
453	### Disconnected-work backfill
454	
455	A worker that started while no server was running may have claimed and
456	finished beads before reaching Connected. To prevent the operator's UI
457	from missing those events:
458	
459	- The worker maintains an in-memory ring buffer of the last N=200
460	  events it emitted while NotConnected (kind, body, bead_id,
461	  attempt_id, timestamp).
462	- On NotConnected → Connected transition, the worker POSTs a
463	  `/api/workers/<id>/backfill` request with the buffered events. Server
464	  acknowledges with 204; worker clears the buffer.
465	- If the buffer overflows (worker generated >200 events while
466	  disconnected — possible during a long outage or many short attempts),
467	  oldest events are dropped silently. The bead's local event log
468	  remains complete; only the server's derived view is incomplete. UI
469	  marks the worker as `had_dropped_backfill: true` so operators know to
470	  consult bead-local logs.
471	- Backfill is best-effort: a 5xx during backfill leaves the buffer
472	  intact; worker tries again on next NotConnected → Connected.
473	
474	### Cancel SLA
475	
476	Operator-initiated cancel writes `extra.cancel-requested: true` to the
477	bead via the server's `/api/beads/<id>/cancel` endpoint (or directly
478	via the bead store). The worker honors at the next safe point:
479	
480	- **Mid-attempt poll** (default every 10s during long attempts): the
481	  worker re-reads the bead's `extra` map. If `cancel-requested: true`
482	  appears, the worker aborts the next LLM turn / next git operation
483	  boundary and reports `preserved_for_review` with reason `operator_cancel`.
484	- **Worst-case latency**: 10s (mid-attempt poll interval) plus the
485	  current LLM turn duration (typically 5-30s). Operators expecting
486	  faster cancel use OS signals.
487	- **Idempotency**: a bead with `cancel-requested: true` already worked
488	  is silently consumed (worker writes `cancel-honored: true` next to
489	  it); a worker starting work on a bead with `cancel-requested: true`
490	  immediately reports `preserved_for_review` and skips the attempt.
491	
492	## Compatibility analysis
493	
494	### Migrates cleanly (almost everything)
495	
496	- **Existing autonomous worker behavior.** This is the baseline rev 4
497	  preserves. Today's `ddx work` and `ddx work --local` already work
498	  against the bead store; rev 4 is additive (best-effort server
499	  reporting on top of unchanged behavior).
500	- **Bead event log shape.** Worker events go to the bead's local event
501	  log (today's behavior); the server-side ingestion endpoint receives a
502	  copy with the same shape. No reader changes.
503	- **Evidence layout.** `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/` unchanged.
504	- **`try.Attempt` semantics.** Unchanged. The worker calls into `try`
505	  exactly as today.
506	- **`requireTrusted` boundary.** Reused for the two new endpoints; no
507	  new auth plane.
508	- **Existing per-Run `attempted` map and stay-alive defaults.** Preserved
509	  from commit `41cb762e`. The autonomous picker is the picker.
510	
511	### Breaking changes for operators
512	
513	- **None for the autonomous path.** `ddx work` and `ddx work --local`
514	  behave identically to today (with the addition of best-effort server
515	  reporting when a server is reachable).
516	- **Some legacy server-spawn integration tests will need updating.** The
517	  current server-spawn path that hand-marshals `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec`
518	  over an internal contract is replaced by the server `exec`'ing
519	  `ddx work` like an operator would. Tests asserting specific spawn
520	  args will need to assert the new exec form. (No operator-visible
521	  behavior change here — operators don't call the legacy endpoint
522	  directly.)
523	- **Per-user direction**: the previous `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` path
524	  through the server is removed; no compat shim and no data migration
525	  for legacy `.ddx/workers/` records (delete history if needed).
526	
527	### Tests that need updating (smaller list than rev 1-3)
528	
529	- `cli/internal/server/workers_test.go` — exec-spawn tests: assert the
530	  new exec form (`ddx work` invocation), not the JSON request/response
531	  envelope.
532	- Integration tests asserting specific `.ddx/workers/` on-disk format
533	  may need adjustment if rev 4 changes that layout. Currently rev 4 does
534	  NOT modify `.ddx/workers/` shape; workers continue to write spec.json
535	  + status.json there for `ddx agent doctor` consumption.
536	- New tests for the two endpoints: `TestWorkerRegister_HappyPath`,
537	  `TestWorkerRegister_ServerDown_WorkerProceeds`,
538	  `TestWorkerEvent_Mirrored_BestEffort`,
539	  `TestWorkerEvent_ServerCrash_WorkerLogs_Continues`.
540	
541	The tests-that-break list is dramatically smaller than rev 1-3 because
542	the worker state machine, bead store interaction, and evidence layout
543	are all unchanged. Only the new server-side ingestion endpoint and its
544	two tests are net-new.
545	
546	## Sequencing relative to in-flight work
547	
548	Rev 4 dramatically simplifies sequencing. The architecture is additive:
549	the worker keeps working as it does today; server-side reporting is new
550	machinery that sits alongside. There is no "freeze C5/C7/C9 until X"
551	rule because nothing about the worker state machine changes.
552	
553	- **`ddx-29058e2a` (ExecuteLoopSpec unification)** — independent. Rev 4
554	  does NOT make ExecuteLoopSpec a wire format. The bead remains valuable
555	  as a worker-side struct unification (cobra → in-process flow) and
556	  should land independently.
557	- **`ddx-5cb6e6cd` C5 (no_changes adjudication)** — NOT FROZEN. Rev 5
558	  doesn't reshape no_changes generation; mirroring stays an observer.
559	- **`ddx-5cb6e6cd` C7 (uniform Guard contract; delete attempted/hookFailed maps)** — **COORDINATE.** Direct conflict: rev 5's worker keeps the per-Run `attempted` map (preserved from commit `41cb762e`); C7 wants to delete it. Resolution: C7 lands BEFORE rev 5 implementation work, OR rev 5 implementation work coordinates with C7's replacement (Guard-derived exclusion). Until coordinated, C7 stays parked.
560	- **`ddx-5cb6e6cd` C9 (StopCondition + cost-cap)** — NOT FROZEN. The autonomous worker keeps the stay-alive defaults; C9's StopCondition becomes an internal worker construct, server sees terminal state via the result event.
561	- **`ddx-dc157075` (stay-alive at commit `41cb762e`)** — already shipped
562	  and preserved. Worker's autonomous behavior plus 30s poll-interval
563	  default carry forward unchanged.
564	- **`ddx-4c51d33e` (cross-project leak at commit `33b97f25`)** — already
565	  shipped. Rev 4 prevents recurrence at the worker level (one worker
566	  per project bead store; no cross-store reads).
567	- **`ddx-9d55601f` (picker priority bug at commit `80f51574`)** — picker
568	  diagnostic events stay; rev 4 explicitly mirrors them to the server
569	  endpoint when the worker is Connected.
570	- **`ddx-9e4c238d` (auto-routing rejection bug, just filed)** —
571	  independent. Fixing DDx's pre-flight to pass through to fizeau is
572	  orthogonal to the autonomous-default architecture. Should land in
573	  parallel.
574	- **`ddx-1d867ec1` (rename `execute_bead_loop.go`)** — coordinate. The
575	  rename should align with whatever package layout the worker uses
576	  post-rev-4. Probably stays as `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`
577	  for now since rev 4 doesn't reshape the worker package; revisit when
578	  C13 lands.
579	- **`ddx-076147ee` (this TD)** — closes when the rev 4 ADR is approved
580	  and the implementation roadmap below is filed as beads.
581	- **`ddx-50da9674` (clean fixture repo)** — supports rev 4's acceptance
582	  test; independent.
583	
584	## Implementation roadmap
585	
586	Rev 5 expands from rev 4's 5 beads to 9, after codex flagged missing
587	UI/doctor/FEAT-006/server-spawn-migration/backfill scope.
588	
589	1. **server: ingestion endpoints** — implement `POST /api/workers/register` + `POST /api/workers/<id>/event` + `POST /api/workers/<id>/backfill`; in-memory derived view backed by append-only `.ddx/server/worker-events.jsonl`; freshness state machine (connected/stale/disconnected); requireTrusted boundary. ~300 LOC.
590	
591	2. **worker: server-probe goroutine + best-effort event mirror** — periodic reachability check on `~/.local/share/ddx/server.addr` (XDG-compliant, matches current code) with jittered 30s interval, immediate first probe, exponential backoff on consecutive failures; transitions emit register/disconnect; in-memory ring buffer for backfill (200-event cap); event POSTs are best-effort. ~250 LOC.
592	
593	3. **server: operator-cancel** — `/api/beads/<id>/cancel` writes `extra.cancel-requested: true`; worker mid-attempt poll (10s default) checks bead `extra` and aborts at next safe point with `preserved_for_review` reason `operator_cancel`; idempotency via `cancel-honored: true`. ~150 LOC.
594	
595	4. **CLI: deprecate `--local`** — flag becomes no-op with deprecation warning; update CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, getting-started, and the cobra help text. Existing tests asserting `--local`-specific behavior get rewritten or deleted. (Codex notes the sweep is larger than 50 LOC: `cli/cmd/work_test.go`, `agent_execute_loop_test.go`, `zero_config_work_test.go`, `skills/ddx/reference/work.md`, demos.) ~150 LOC + doc edits.
596	
597	5. **server: derived-view GraphQL + UI workers panel** — schema fields: `workers { id, project, harness, state, last_event_at, mirror_failures_count, had_dropped_backfill, current_bead, current_attempt }`; UI surfaces freshness indicators + duplicate-worker display + the "trusted-peer reported, not authoritative" labeling. Existing workers panel migrated. ~400 LOC frontend + ~100 LOC backend.
598	
599	6. **`ddx agent doctor` migration** — read worker state from server's runtime registry when available; fall back to `.ddx/workers/` on-disk files when no server. The on-disk format stays as the fallback-source-of-truth for one alpha release lag, then deprecated. ~150 LOC.
600	
601	7. **server-spawn path migration** — `cli/internal/server/workers.go` and `handleStartExecuteLoopWorker` switch from hand-marshalled spec to `exec ddx work` with env-vars. The legacy spec serialization deletes when this lands. ~200 LOC + test updates.
602	
603	8. **FEAT-006 amendment** — replace the rev 3 worker-contract section with rev 5's autonomous-default + best-effort-mirror description. Drop session-token/heartbeat/`next-bead`/result/disconnect contract; add register/event/backfill/cancel + freshness state. ~200 lines docs.
604	
605	9. **acceptance + soak** — uses `ddx-50da9674` clean fixture repo. Multi-worker drain WITH server; restart server mid-flight; verify worker continues + reconnects + backfills. Then drain with NO server; verify identical behavior. Operator-cancel test (write marker, observe `preserved_for_review` with `operator_cancel` reason within SLA). Soak: ≥1 week of normal operator use. Final bead.
606	
607	Plus one COORDINATION bead, NOT new work but a parking-lot for the
608	C7 conflict:
609	
610	10. **coordinate: C7 (Guard contract, delete attempted map) with rev 5 worker** — either C7 lands first and rev 5 implementation uses Guard-derived exclusion, OR C7 stays parked until rev 5's worker code defines its replacement for the per-Run attempted map. Decision documented; no LOC.
611	
612	Total estimated effort: 2-4 weeks (vs 1-2 weeks for rev 4's understated 5-bead roadmap, vs 8-10 weeks for rev 3's design).
613	
614	## Consequences
615	
616	- **Workers don't depend on the server.** The headline operator
617	  requirement (server restart preserves in-flight work) is satisfied
618	  by construction: workers don't notice server restarts as anything
619	  more than transient mirror failures.
620	- **No `--local` flag.** Removed from the CLI surface. Behavior of
621	  today's `--local` becomes the always-on default. Backward compat
622	  preserved for one release via a no-op flag with deprecation warning.
623	- **Server is opt-in observability.** Operators who don't run a server
624	  see no behavior change from today. Operators who do see
625	  centralized event aggregation, cross-worker visibility, and an
626	  operator UI. Both are correct configurations.
627	- **`ExecuteLoopSpec` unification still happens.** Per `ddx-29058e2a` —
628	  the spec is a worker-side struct, not a wire format. The drop class
629	  is closed by single-struct discipline; it just doesn't need to cross
630	  a network boundary.
631	- **No claim coordination races.** Bead store's atomic claim is the
632	  only claim primitive. Two workers contending use the same store
633	  CAS that has worked correctly for as long as DDx has had a bead
634	  store.
635	- **No "kill server → workers exit" tests left valid.** Any test that
636	  relied on this behavior gets updated to assert "kill server →
637	  workers continue working autonomously."
638	- **Server restart loses derived view briefly.** The server's view of
639	  who's working what comes back within one probe cycle (default 30s)
640	  as workers re-register. No in-flight work is at risk.
641	- **Operator UX for cancel changes slightly.** Today operators kill
642	  worker subprocesses or wait. After: operators send cancel via the
643	  server (or write the marker to the bead store directly); worker
644	  honors at next safe point. Documented in CHANGELOG.
645	- **Evidence layout unchanged.** `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/` still
646	  receives all per-attempt artifacts; worker writes there exactly as
647	  today.

23	func NewFixtureRepo(t *testing.T, profile string) string {

18	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
19	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
20	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on
21	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
22	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
23		Log          io.Writer
24		EventSink    io.Writer
25		ProgressCh   chan<- ProgressEvent
26		PreClaimHook func(ctx context.Context) error
27		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, is invoked between nextCandidate and
28		// Claim. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute against the
29		// loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed routing
30		// error the upstream surfaced — notably agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible
31		// when the harness allow-list rejects the model. Any non-nil error
32		// causes the loop to exit immediately without claiming the bead, with
33		// a worker-level execution_failed record naming the rejected pair.
34		// DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list; this gate only
35		// consumes the typed-incompatibility surface.
36		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
37		Once           bool
38		PollInterval   time.Duration
39		NoReview       bool
40		LabelFilter    string
41		SessionID      string
42		WorkerID       string
43		ProjectRoot    string
44		// WakeCh, when non-nil, signals the idle-poll sleep to return immediately
45		// so the loop re-scans the queue. Used by the operator-prompt approve /
46		// auto-approve mutations (Story 15) to avoid a poll-interval-sized delay
47		// before a freshly-approved bead is claimed. Implementations must send
48		// non-blocking (server-side helpers do); the loop only waits for a
49		// receive on WakeCh during the idle sleep, never elsewhere.
50		WakeCh <-chan struct{}
51	}
52	
53	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
54	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
55	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
56	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
57	
58	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
59	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
60	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
61	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
62	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
63	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
64	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
65	
66	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
67	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
68	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
69	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads

76	// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:
77	// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the
78	// worker.
79	const StoreErrorCooldown = 5 * time.Minute

# build-fixture-repo

Reusable clean ddx-initialized git repo for tests, integration runs, and
acceptance demos. Avoids polluting the main DDx project when an example
workspace is needed.

## Usage

```bash
scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh <dest> [--profile minimal|standard|multi-project|federated]
```

Cleanup is the caller's responsibility (`rm -rf <dest>`).

### Env

| Var       | Purpose                                                  | Default            |
| --------- | -------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------ |
| `DDX_BIN` | Path to the `ddx` binary used for seeding sample beads. | `ddx` (from PATH) |

## Profiles

| Profile         | Layout                                          | Seeded data                                  |
| --------------- | ----------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| `minimal`       | `<dest>/.ddx/` (config + empty `beads.jsonl`)   | none                                         |
| `standard`      | `<dest>/.ddx/`                                  | 5 mixed-priority sample beads (P0–P3)        |
| `multi-project` | `<dest>/proj-a/`, `<dest>/proj-b/`              | `proj-a` seeded with the standard 5 beads    |
| `federated`     | `<dest>/hub/`, `<dest>/spoke/`                  | `hub` seeded with the standard 5 beads. The federation handshake is **not** registered — wire it up in your test if needed. |

Each project is its own git repo (`git init`, initial commit on `main`).

## Examples

```bash
# Operator walkthrough.
scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh /tmp/ddx-demo --profile standard
cd /tmp/ddx-demo && ddx bead ready

# Cross-project smoke.
scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh /tmp/ddx-mp --profile multi-project
ls /tmp/ddx-mp   # proj-a proj-b
```

## Go test helper

Tests should use `testutils.NewFixtureRepo` instead of calling the script
directly — it auto-cleans via `t.Cleanup` and lazily builds a `ddx` binary
from `cli/` if neither `$DDX_BIN` nor `ddx` on `PATH` is available:

```go
import "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/testutils"

func TestX(t *testing.T) {
    projA := testutils.NewFixtureRepo(t, "minimal")
    // ... use projA as a real ddx-initialized project root.
}
```

For `multi-project` / `federated`, the returned path is the parent dir;
sub-projects live underneath (`proj-a`, `proj-b` / `hub`, `spoke`).

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(agent): worker server-probe goroutine + best-effort event mirror [ddx-98257c6a]

ADR-022 rev 5 §Probe + freshness state model, step 2. Workers stay
autonomous against the bead store; in parallel a Probe goroutine
periodically checks ~/.local/share/ddx/server.addr (XDG-compliant) and,
on NotConnected→Connected, registers via /api/workers/register and
mirrors per-attempt loop events to /api/workers/{id}/event best-effort.

Cadence: immediate first probe, then 30s ± 20% jitter; ≥5 consecutive
failures stretch the next interval to MaxInterval (5min cap); a successful
probe resets to base. NotConnected events buffer in a 200-entry ring;
overflow drops oldest and flips HadDroppedBackfill so operators consult
bead-local logs. /api/workers/{id}/backfill flushes the ring on reconnect.

ddx work --local wires the probe via a TeeJSONL adapter on top of the
existing loopSink: every writeLoopEvent JSONL line tees through the
probe as a workerprobe.Event, no changes to the loop body.

Tests:
- TestWorker_ProbeImmediateFirst, TestWorker_ProbeJitter,
  TestWorker_BackfillOnReconnect, TestWorker_BackoffOnFailures,
  TestWorker_NoServer_StillWorks (unit, fake server).
- TestWorker_RealAttemptEvents_FlowToServer (wired-in integration):
  builds a fresh ddx, stands up real serverpkg.Server behind
  httptest, points the worker at it via XDG_DATA_HOME server.addr,
  spawns ddx work --local --once against a standard fixture, and
  asserts loop.start + bead.claimed land in
  .ddx/server/worker-events.jsonl within 5s.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

data.text:
Commit landed at `07c29514`. AC verification:

| AC | Evidence |
|---|---|
| 1. Probe goroutine; cadence + jitter + backoff | `probe.go:run/tick`, `applyJitter`; `TestWorker_ProbeImmediateFirst`, `TestWorker_ProbeJitter`, `TestWorker_BackoffOnFailures` |
| 2. Register on NotConnected→Connected; clear worker_id on Connected→NotConnected | `probe.go:markConnected/markDisconnected` (clears `workerID`) |
| 3. Ring buffer (200 cap; oldest dropped; HadDroppedBackfill flag) | `probe.go:appendBufferLocked`; `TestWorker_NoServer_StillWorks` overflow check |
| 4. Best-effort event mirror; failures logged + continue | `postEvent` re-buffers + `markDisconnected` on failure; `Logger` writes |
| 5. Five named tests | All present in `probe_test.go`, all pass |
| 6. `go test ./internal/agent/...` green | Verified (110s sequential run, all green) |
| 7. Wired-in integration test | `TestWorker_RealAttemptEvents_FlowToServer` — passes; observed `loop.start` + `bead.claimed` flowing through real subprocess → real server |

Worker server-probe goroutine + event mirror is wired through the `ddx work --local` path via a JSONL tee on the existing loopSink; integration test proves the end-to-end flow.
